name: Roasted Garlic
recipeInstructions:
1 Preheat oven to 375°F.
2 Remove the papery outer covering from each whole garlic head, but do not separate or peel the cloves.
3 Wrap as many whole heads of garlic in heavy-duty aluminum foil so that the cloves are completely enclosed.
4 Bake in the preheated oven for 1 hour and 15 minutes.
5 Serve each diner a head of garlic with some bread and butter. Separate the cloves from the head.
6 Hold a clove over a piece of buttered bread and squeeze gently. The garlic puree will pop out, similar to toothpaste from a tube.
7 Spread and eat.
Garlic Puree:
1 Let roasted heads of garlic cool for at least 5 minutes without unwrapping.
2 Gently separate the cloves and place them over a fine-mesh sieve.
3 Squeeze each clove over the sieve so that the softened garlic enters it.
4 Using a wooden spatula or spoon, rub the garlic through the sieve into a small container or bowl.
5 Cover tightly with plastic wrap and refrigerate until needed. The puree will keep for months.
Quick Puree:
1 If in a hurry, simply squeeze the cloves one by one over a bowl without using the sieve.
2 Use a rubber spatula to push the puree into a neat mound.
3 Cover tightly with plastic wrap and refrigerate until needed. It will keep for months. For indefinite storage, cover with a film of olive oil.
